briefdescription.para: Sets the XV_Mix_l2 instance to its power-on default state.

detaileddescription.para:
This function initializes the video mixer instance to a known default state after power-on or reset. The following actions are performed:
Disables all video layers.
Sets up a default video stream configuration with custom mode, color format, frame rate, color depth, and active video dimensions based on the IP configuration.
Applies the default video stream to the mixer.
Sets the default background color to blue with the configured color depth.
For each layer, if scaling is disabled, updates the layer's maximum width to the IP's maximum width.
Enables only the master (stream) layer.
Disables interrupts to set up polling mode.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_Mix_l2 instance to be initialized.

simplesect.para: None

briefdescription.para: Checks if a given video window is valid within the bounds of a video stream, considering a specified scaling factor.

detaileddescription.para: This function creates a copy of the provided window, applies the scaling factor to its width and height, and then verifies whether the scaled window fits within the active area of the video stream.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the video stream structure containing timing info.
Pointer to the video window structure to validate.
Scaling factor to apply to the window dimensions.

simplesect.para: TRUE if the scaled window fits within the stream's active area, FALSE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: XVMix_Initialize - Initialize the XV_Mix_l2 instance for SDT flow.

detaileddescription.para: This function initializes the XV_Mix_l2 instance specified by InstancePtr using the hardware configuration at the given BaseAddress. It first asserts that the provided pointer is not NULL, clears the instance memory, and then calls the lower-level XV_mix_Initialize function. If initialization is successful, it sets the instance to its power-on default state.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_Mix_l2 instance to be initialized.
Base address of the hardware configuration to use.

listitem.para: Error code defined by XV_mix_Initialize otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Enables interrupts for the XV_Mix_l2 instance.

detaileddescription.para:
This function performs the following actions:
Asserts that the provided instance pointer is not NULL.
Enables the DONE interrupt for the underlying XV_mix hardware.
Globally enables interrupts for the XV_mix hardware.
Disables the auto-restart feature of the XV_mix hardware.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_Mix_l2 instance.

briefdescription.para: XVMix_InterruptDisable - Disables interrupts for the XV_Mix_l2 instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function disables the DONE interrupt and the global interrupt for the specified XV_Mix_l2 instance. After disabling interrupts, it enables the auto-restart feature to allow the hardware to automatically restart processing without requiring software intervention.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_Mix_l2 instance.

simplesect.para: The InstancePtr must not be NULL.

briefdescription.para: Starts the video mixer hardware.

detaileddescription.para: This function asserts that the provided instance pointer is not NULL, and then starts the underlying XV_mix hardware by calling its Start function.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_Mix_l2 instance to be started.

briefdescription.para: Stops the XV_Mix_l2 core operation.

detaileddescription.para: This function disables the autostart feature, initiates a flush operation, and waits for the flush to complete or until a timeout occurs.

InstancePtr
Pointer to the XV_Mix_l2 instance.
The function performs the following steps:
Asserts that the instance pointer is not NULL.
Disables the autostart mode of the core.
Sets the flush bit to initiate a flush operation.
Waits for the flush operation to complete by polling the flush done status, with a timeout to prevent indefinite waiting.
Returns early if the flush operation does not complete within the timeout.

briefdescription.para: Sets the video stream configuration for the mixer instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function copies the provided video stream structure into the mixer instance, and programs the hardware registers for the active video width and height.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_Mix_l2 instance.
Pointer to the input XVidC_VideoStream structure.

simplesect.para: The function asserts that the input pointers are valid and that the resolution is within the supported range.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the specified layer of the core instance.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is layer number to be enabled

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS or XST_FAILURE
To enable all layers use layer id XVMIX_LAYER_ALL

briefdescription.para: This function disables the specified layer of the core instance.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is layer number to be disabled

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS or XST_FAILURE
To disable all layers use layer id XVMIX_LAYER_ALL

briefdescription.para: This function returns state of the specified layer [enabled or disabled].

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the core instance.
is the layer index for which information is requested

simplesect.para: Enabled(1)/Disabled(0)

briefdescription.para: This function sets the background color to be displayed when stream layer is disabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is requested background color
is color depth

simplesect.para: none

briefdescription.para: This function configures the window coordinates of the specified layer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is the layer for which window coordinates are to be set
is the window coordinates in pixels

is the stride of the requested window yuv422 Color space requires 2 Bytes/Pixel yuv444 Color space requires 4 Bytes/Pixel Equation to compute stride is as follows Stride = (Window_Width * (YUV422 ? 2 : 4)) (Applicable only when layer type is Memory)

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if command is successful else error code with reason
Applicable only for Layer1-8 and Logo Layer

briefdescription.para: This function read the window coordinates of the specified layer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is the layer for which window coordinates are to be set
is the pointer to return window coordinates

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if command is successful else error code with reason
Applicable only for Layer1-8 and Logo Layer

memberdef.argsstring: (XV_Mix_l2 *InstancePtr, XVMix_LayerId LayerId, u16 StartX, u16 StartY)
memberdef.name: XVMix_MoveLayerWindow

briefdescription.para: This function moved the window position of the specified layer to new coordinates.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is the layer for which window position is to be set
is the new X position
is the new Y position

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if command is successful else error code with reason
Applicable only for Layer1-8 and Logo Layer

briefdescription.para: This function configures the scaling factor of the specified layer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is the layer to be updated
is the scale factor

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if command is successful else error code with reason
Applicable only for Layer1-8 and Logo Layer

briefdescription.para: This function returns the scaling factor of the specified layer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is the layer for which information is requested

simplesect.para: If layer enabled, current set value, else ~0
Applicable only for Layer1-8 and Logo Layer

briefdescription.para: This function configures the Alpha level of the specified layer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is the layer to be updated
is the new value

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if command is successful else error code with reason
Applicable only for Layer1-8 and Logo Layer

briefdescription.para: This function returns the alpha of the specified layer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is the layer for which information is requested

simplesect.para: If layer enabled, current set value, else ~0
Applicable only for Layer1-8 and Logo Layer

briefdescription.para: This function reads the color format of the specified layer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is the layer to be updated
is pointer to color format return variable

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS or XST_FAILURE
none

briefdescription.para: This function sets the buffer address of the specified layer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is the layer to be updated
is the absolute address of buffer in memory

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS or XST_FAILURE
Applicable only for Layer1-16

briefdescription.para: This function reads the buffer address of the specified layer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is the layer to be updated

simplesect.para: Address of buffer in memory
Applicable only for Layer1-16

briefdescription.para: This function sets the buffer address of the specified layer for the UV plane for semi-planar formats for the U plane for 3-planar formats.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is the layer to be updated
is the absolute address of second buffer in memory

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS or XST_FAILURE
Applicable only for Layer1-16

briefdescription.para: This function reads the buffer address of the specified layer for the UV plane for semi-planar formats and for the U plane for 3-planar formats.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to core instance to be worked upon
is the layer to be updated

simplesect.para: Address of second buffer in memory
Applicable only for Layer1-8

briefdescription.para: Set the chroma buffer address for the specified layer.

detaileddescription.para: This function sets the address of the second chroma buffer for a given layer in the XV_Mix_l2 instance. It checks for valid input parameters, ensures the address is properly aligned according to the AXI memory interface requirements, and writes the address to the appropriate hardware register. The function updates the internal state of the instance with the new buffer address.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_Mix_l2 instance.
ID of the layer for which the chroma buffer address is to be set. Must be greater than XVMIX_LAYER_MASTER and less than XVMIX_LAYER_LOGO.
Physical address of the chroma buffer. Must be non-zero and properly aligned.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the address was set successfully.
XVMIX_ERR_MEM_ADDR_MISALIGNED if the address is not properly aligned.
XST_FAILURE for other failures (e.g., invalid parameters).

simplesect.para: The address must be aligned to (2 * PixPerClk * 4) bytes. The function does not perform any memory allocation or buffer management.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the address of the second chroma buffer for a specified layer in the video mixer instance.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_Mix_l2 instance.
Identifier of the layer for which the chroma buffer address is requested. Must be greater than XVMIX_LAYER_MASTER and less than XVMIX_LAYER_LOGO.

simplesect.para: The address (UINTPTR) of the second chroma buffer for the specified layer. Returns 0 if the LayerId is invalid or out of range.

The function asserts that InstancePtr is not NULL and that LayerId is within the valid range. The function only returns a valid address if LayerId is less than the number of layers configured in the instance.

briefdescription.para: Set the logo color key values for the XV_Mix_l2 instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function configures the minimum and maximum RGB values for the logo color key, which are used to enable color keying (transparency) for the logo overlay in the video mixer. The function only sets the color key values if both the logo and logo color key features are enabled in the instance.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_Mix_l2 instance.
Structure containing the minimum and maximum RGB values for the color key.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the color key values were set successfully.
XST_FAILURE if the logo or logo color key feature is not enabled.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the current logo color key range from the hardware.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
Pointer to the XV_Mix_l2 instance.
ColorKeyData
Pointer to an XVMix_LogoColorKey structure where the color key minimum and maximum RGB values will be stored.
This function checks if the logo and logo color key features are enabled. If both are enabled, it reads the minimum and maximum RGB values for the logo color key from the hardware registers and stores them in the provided ColorKeyData structure.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if the color key values were successfully retrieved, XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Loads a logo image into the mixer hardware.

detaileddescription.para: This function takes separate R, G, and B buffers representing a logo image, and writes the pixel data into the hardware registers of the video mixer for display as a logo overlay. The function supports logos with dimensions and alignment constraints as specified by the hardware configuration.

InstancePtr
Pointer to the XV_Mix_l2 instance.
Win
Pointer to an XVidC_VideoWindow structure specifying the position and size of the logo window.
RBuffer
Pointer to the buffer containing the red channel data.
GBuffer
Pointer to the buffer containing the green channel data.
BBuffer
Pointer to the buffer containing the blue channel data.
XST_SUCCESS if the logo was loaded successfully.
XST_FAILURE if the logo could not be loaded (e.g., invalid parameters or logo not enabled).
Preconditions:
The logo feature must be enabled in the mixer hardware.
The window dimensions and alignment must meet hardware requirements.
The input buffers must be valid and sized appropriately for the window.

Side Effects:
Writes logo pixel data to hardware registers.
Updates the logo layer buffer pointers in the instance.

briefdescription.para: XVMix_LoadLogoPixelAlpha - Loads per-pixel alpha values for a logo window into the hardware.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
Pointer to the XV_Mix_l2 instance.
Win
Pointer to the XVidC_VideoWindow structure specifying the logo window position and size.
ABuffer
Pointer to the buffer containing alpha values for each pixel in the logo window.
This function loads the per-pixel alpha values from the provided buffer into the hardware registers for the specified logo window. The function checks that the logo and per-pixel alpha features are enabled, and that the window parameters are within valid ranges. The alpha values are packed into 32-bit words (4 pixels per word) and written to the hardware.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if the operation is successful, XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: XVMix_DbgReportStatus - Prints the current status and configuration of the mixer instance.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
Pointer to the XV_Mix_l2 instance to report status for.
This function outputs detailed information about the mixer hardware and its configuration to the standard output using xil_printf. The reported information includes:
Pixels per clock
Color depth
Number of layers
Control register value
Layer enable register value
Enable/disable status of each layer (Master, Layer 1-N, Logo)
Background color values (Y/R, U/G, V/B)
The function asserts that the provided instance pointer is not NULL. It is intended for debugging and diagnostic purposes.

briefdescription.para: Prints debug information for a specific layer in the video mixer.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
Pointer to the XV_Mix_l2 instance.
LayerId
Identifier of the layer to report (master, logo, or regular layer).
This function prints detailed status and configuration information for the specified layer in the video mixer. The information includes:
Whether the layer is enabled or disabled.
For the master layer: color format, resolution, and stream info.
For the logo layer: alpha, scale factor, window position/size, color key data, and pixel alpha status.
For regular layers: interface type (memory/stream), buffer addresses, alpha and scaling status/values, color format, window position/size, and stride.
The function uses xil_printf for output and is intended for debugging purposes. Assertions are used to validate input parameters.

simplesect.para: none

memberdef.argsstring: (XV_Mix_l2 *InstancePtr, XVidC_ColorStd ColorStd, XVidC_ColorRange colorRange, u8 colorDepth)
memberdef.name: XVMix_SetCoeffForYuvToRgb

briefdescription.para: Sets the coefficients for YUV to RGB color space conversion in the XV_Mix hardware.

detaileddescription.para: This function configures the color space conversion (CSC) matrix coefficients for converting YUV to RGB based on the specified color standard, color range, and color depth. The coefficients are written to the hardware registers of the XV_Mix instance.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_Mix_l2 instance.
The color standard to use (e.g., BT.601, BT.709).
The color range (e.g., full range, limited range).
The color depth in bits per component (e.g., 8, 10, 12).

simplesect.para: none
Applicable for all 16 Layers

memberdef.argsstring: (XV_Mix_l2 *InstancePtr, XVidC_ColorStd ColorStd, XVidC_ColorRange colorRange, u8 colorDepth)
memberdef.name: XVMix_SetCoeffForRgbToYuv

briefdescription.para: Sets the RGB to YUV color space conversion coefficients for the mixer instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function configures the color space conversion (CSC) matrix coefficients in the hardware registers of the mixer to perform RGB to YUV conversion. The coefficients are selected based on the specified color standard, color range, and color depth. The function writes the scaled coefficients to the appropriate hardware registers.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_Mix_l2 instance.
The color standard to use (e.g., BT.601, BT.709).
The color range (e.g., full range, limited range).
The color depth (bits per component).

memberdef.argsstring: (XV_Mix_l2 *InstancePtr, XVidC_ColorStd colorStandard, XVidC_ColorRange colorRange, u8 colorDepth)
memberdef.name: XVMix_SetCscCoeffs

briefdescription.para: Sets the Color Space Conversion (CSC) coefficients for the XV_Mix_l2 instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function configures the CSC coefficients based on the specified color standard, color range, and color depth. It first checks if the provided color standard and color range are supported. If the CSC coefficient registers are enabled in the hardware, it sets the coefficients for both YUV-to-RGB and RGB-to-YUV conversions. If the registers are not enabled, it returns an error indicating that the feature is not available.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the XV_Mix_l2 instance.
Specifies the color standard (e.g., BT.601, BT.709).
Specifies the color range (e.g., full, limited).
Specifies the color depth (in bits).

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the coefficients are set successfully.
XST_FAILURE if an unknown color standard or color range is provided.
XST_NO_FEATURE if the CSC coefficient registers are not enabled.
